test_cases.title: Cart badge appears showing 1 after adding a single product
test_cases.description: From an empty cart on the inventory page, adding one product makes the cart badge appear with count 1.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The badge's appearance and its rendered count are a visible result of a UI journey (add-to-cart on the inventory page). There is no separate cart API to assert against for SauceDemo, so the only faithful check is the rendered badge after the real interaction. Smoke / regression critical — this is the primary happy path of the feature.
preconditions: Shopper is signed in as standard_user on https://www.saucedemo.com.
Shopper is on the inventory page with an empty cart (no badge visible).

steps.action: Confirm no cart badge is visible on the inventory page.
Click 'Add to cart' on the first product (Sauce Labs Backpack).

expected_results: The cart badge shows the text '1'.

test_cases.title: Cart badge shows 2 after adding two distinct products
test_cases.description: Adding two different products causes the cart badge to show count 2.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Verifies the badge counts distinct items correctly, not just toggles between empty and one. The count arithmetic is only observable through the rendered badge after two real add actions; no API surface exists to assert it directly.
preconditions: Shopper is signed in as standard_user on the inventory page with an empty cart.

steps.action: Click 'Add to cart' on the first product (Sauce Labs Backpack).
Click 'Add to cart' on a second, distinct product (Sauce Labs Bike Light).

expected_results: The cart badge shows the text '2'.

test_cases.title: Cart badge decrements on remove and disappears when cart returns to empty
test_cases.description: Removing products from the inventory page decrements the badge, and the badge disappears entirely once the cart is empty again.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The disappearance of the badge at zero is a state-transition that can only be confirmed visually after a real remove action; an assertion that an element is NOT present is exactly the kind of UI guarantee E2E exists for. High priority because a phantom badge (count stuck above zero) directly misleads the shopper — RISK-002.
preconditions: Shopper is signed in as standard_user on the inventory page.
Two distinct products have been added (badge shows '2').

steps.action: Click 'Remove' on the first added product (Sauce Labs Backpack).
Confirm the cart badge now shows '1'.
Click 'Remove' on the second added product (Sauce Labs Bike Light).

expected_results: After the first remove the cart badge shows '1'.
After the second remove the cart badge is no longer visible (cart is empty).

test_cases.title: Cart badge count survives navigation to the cart page and back
test_cases.description: Navigating to the cart page and back to the inventory page preserves the badge count.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Navigation persistence is a cross-page journey assertion: only a real round-trip (inventory -> cart -> inventory) exercises whether client state is retained. Cannot be checked at a single page or via a unit test of one component.
preconditions: Shopper is signed in as standard_user on the inventory page.
Two distinct products have been added (badge shows '2').

steps.action: Click the cart icon to navigate to the cart page.
Click 'Continue Shopping' to return to the inventory page.

expected_results: On returning to the inventory page the cart badge still shows '2'.
